name: product-ux
description: BeSpunky product & UX design skills. keep-users-oriented: whenever you make someone wait or move them through a process, answer the three questions - what is the expected result, where am I in the process, what is the next step - and pick the right feedback for deterministic vs nondeterministic processes. A universal service-design principle, expressed primarily through software UI (loading/progress, async ops, multi-step flows, long-running jobs, notifications, optimistic UI). envision-the-experience: imagine the world an interface lives in - its feeling, place, and metaphor - before any layout or component, and design every element as a native inhabitant of that world rather than a generic control on a page; the screen is a place, not a page. Interrogate every element the spec names (a 'menu' might become a sunflower whose petals you pick), speak in sensory/non-technical language naming no implementation, keep immersive from becoming busy (restraint over spectacle), and produce a Vision. realize-the-vision: the craftsman that turns a Vision into a real, running interface by RESEARCHING the truest, soundest means before writing any code - reads the Vision as a contract (the feeling must survive), surveys the field (GSAP, Motion, three.js/R3F/angular-three, Web Animations, CSS scroll-driven animations, View Transitions, Lottie/Rive, Canvas/SVG/WebGL, Web Audio, haptics), weighs tradeoffs and caveats (jank, layout thrash, reduced-motion, accessibility, mobile), and knows when to build in code vs. when to SOURCE an asset - figurative/photoreal art (a couple kissing, a landscape) is generated, licensed, or downloaded, never hand-coded into path-soup, handling licensing, review, and art-direction. Chooses deliberately for the vision AND the constraints, designs and confirms the architecture before building (architecture-first), wraps imperative libraries behind clean Angular seams, and verifies against the feeling in the running app. Routes to a reference library (motion & timelines; depth/3D/spatial; scroll & cinematic; SVG/canvas/generative; build-vs-source & sourcing/generating assets; performance & budgets; accessibility/reduced-motion/fallbacks; sound & haptics). The matched building half of envision-the-experience.
